title: Decision Tree - Aggressive / Violent Behavior Response (Code White)

N0.prompt: Step 1: A violence situation or behavior likely to lead to violence is observed. What should be done first?

options.text: Immediately notify the Security Operations Room, Security Supervisor, Security Manager, and shift manager.
Wait to see whether the situation becomes physical before notifying anyone.
options.feedback: Immediate notification of the internal security chain is required.

N1.prompt: Step 2: After receiving the report, what is the first operational action?

options.text: Rapidly verify the source of the report and the exact incident location.
Delay verification until leadership arrives.
options.feedback: Rapid verification of source and location is required immediately.

N2.prompt: Step 3: How should security deal with persons showing behavioral or emotional disturbance?

options.text: Exercise caution and monitor their movements closely.
Ignore them unless an injury occurs.
options.feedback: The policy requires caution and active monitoring of movements.

N3.prompt: Step 4: What information action is required?

options.text: Collect available relevant information and report it to the Security Operations Room or Security Supervisor.
Avoid collecting information until the case closes.
options.feedback: Available information should be collected and reported through the proper chain.

N4.prompt: Step 5: When should additional security support be requested?

options.text: Whenever the assessed situation requires additional support.
Only after the incident is fully over.
options.feedback: Security reinforcement should be requested whenever the incident requires it.

N5.prompt: Step 6: How should Code White be activated?

options.text: Announce Code White through the overhead speaker system and signal the response teams through wireless communication devices.
Inform one guard only and avoid wider activation.
options.feedback: Formal internal announcement and signaling of response teams are required.

N6.prompt: Step 7: What staffing response is required after Code White activation?

options.text: Provide additional security support according to the type and severity of the aggression.
Reduce visible staffing to avoid attention.
options.feedback: Security support should be increased according to severity and type.

N7.prompt: Step 8: When should specialized authorities be contacted?

options.text: Immediately, depending on the type of aggression and the extent to which the incident can be controlled internally.
Only after the aggressor is fully controlled internally.
options.feedback: Specialized authorities may need to be contacted immediately based on incident type and controllability.

N8.prompt: Step 9: What is required regarding facility leadership?

options.text: Notify facility leadership immediately and provide continuous updates on developments.
Notify leadership after final paperwork only.
options.feedback: Leadership must be informed immediately and kept updated.

N9.prompt: Step 10: What protective action is required in the incident area?

options.text: Protect workers, isolate them from the aggressor when possible, and prevent crowd gathering around the site.
Allow companions and bystanders to gather nearby so they can help.
options.feedback: Workers must be protected and crowd gathering must be prevented.

N10.prompt: Step 11: What should be done regarding injured persons?

options.text: Attempt to reach injured persons with medical staff for safe transfer while maintaining high safety standards.
Delay all medical access until all reporting is complete.
options.feedback: Safe access to injured persons should be attempted with medical staff when conditions permit.

N11.prompt: Step 12: If dialogue with the aggressor is possible, how should it be handled?

options.text: Engage only when appropriate, aiming to calm and de-escalate the situation while avoiding provocation.
Use strong confrontational language to force a quick reaction.
options.feedback: Dialogue is used for de-escalation, not provocation.

N12.prompt: Step 13: When should female security staff be requested?

options.text: If the nature of the situation or the persons involved requires it.
Only after the case is officially closed.
options.feedback: Female security support may be needed during the active response.

N13.prompt: Step 14: What environmental and support measures are required in the incident area?

options.text: Remove hazardous objects when safe and seek support from Social Work in communication and control.
Leave all objects in place and avoid coordination with other departments.
options.feedback: Potential violence tools should be removed when safe, and Social Work support may assist with control.

N14.prompt: Step 15: If the violence escalates into direct assault, what should security do?

options.text: Intervene to control the aggressor, and if the aggressor has a bladed weapon or firearm, deal cautiously and calmly until specialized authorities arrive.
Withdraw all support immediately and stop monitoring the incident.
options.feedback: Escalation requires intervention to control the aggressor, with extra caution if weapons are involved.

N15.prompt: Step 16: What is the correct action after attempting control of the aggressor?

options.text: If controlled without resistance, complete formal procedures; if not controlled, request support, protect people present, and wait for reinforcement and specialized authorities.
End the response immediately whether or not the aggressor is controlled.
options.feedback: The next step depends on whether the aggressor is successfully controlled.

N16.prompt: Step 17: What should be done if the incident moves beyond control?

options.text: Establish a security perimeter and allow only trained personnel to perform any necessary force in accordance with approved procedures and regulations.
Allow any available person to help restrain the aggressor.
options.feedback: Escalated incidents require perimeter control and only trained lawful intervention.

N17.prompt: Step 18: How should people present in the incident area be managed?

options.text: Keep them calm, guide them to safe places and emergency exits away from danger, and close main entrances except for specialized authorities.
Allow everyone to move freely without direction.
options.feedback: People present should be guided calmly toward safety and access should be controlled.

N18.prompt: Step 19: What is the CCTV team expected to do during the incident?

options.text: Monitor the incident, track the aggressor’s movements, and direct relevant response personnel based on developments.
Stop monitoring until the incident is over.
options.feedback: CCTV monitoring continues during the incident to support response coordination.

N19.prompt: Step 20: What documentation is required after the incident?

options.text: Prepare an incident registration report and a joint internal incident record involving the required departments, then submit them to facility leadership.
Document the case verbally only if someone asks later.
options.feedback: Formal written reporting and a joint incident record are required.

N20.prompt: Step 21: What should be obtained after the specialized authorities complete the investigation and close the case?

options.text: A copy of the final report including observations and recommendations.
No follow-up documentation is needed after case closure.
options.feedback: A final report copy should be obtained after the investigation is closed.

N21.prompt: Step 22: What is the final external communication requirement?

options.text: Provide the National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management with all required incident information through the responsible facility authority.
Close the incident once the internal record is submitted, with no further communication.
options.feedback: The National Center must receive the required final incident information.

options.text: Restart
